name: Maytham al-Tammar
title: Eloquent Tongue of Truth
shortDescription: The confidant of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) who was hanged in Kufa before the event of Ashura due to his love for Ali (AS).
fullStory:
Maytham al-Tammar was one of the closest and most trusted companions of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS). He was a simple date seller in Kufa, but he possessed knowledge of hidden secrets and future events because Ali (AS) had taught him these sciences.

Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) had informed him that one day, because of his love for him, his hands and feet would be cut off, he would be hanged, and his tongue would be cut out. Maytham eagerly awaited that day and even knew the palm tree he was to be hanged on, speaking to it.

A few days before Imam Hussain (AS) arrived in Karbala, Ibn Ziyad, fearing the influence of Maytham's words, arrested him and demanded he disassociate from Ali (AS). Maytham refused and, from the top of the gallows, began recounting the virtues of Ali (AS).

Seeing that Maytham wouldn't stop enlightening the people even on the gallows, Ibn Ziyad ordered his tongue to be cut out, and he was unjustly martyred a few days before Ashura.
quote: (Amir al-Mu'minin's prophecy to him:) By God, they will cut off your hands and feet and crucify you... and you will be with me in Paradise.
